cards.front: What are the four feedback topologies and their amplifier types?
cards.back: Series-Shunt → Voltage (V→V). Series-Series → Transconductance (V→I). Shunt-Shunt → Transresistance (I→V). Shunt-Series → Current (I→I).

cards.front: What does 'Series' at the input mean?
cards.back: Series mixing: feedback voltage is subtracted in series with the input voltage. V_BE = V_B - V_E. Increases input impedance (R_if = R_i × D).

cards.front: What does 'Shunt' at the input mean?
cards.back: Shunt mixing: feedback current is injected in parallel at the input node. Decreases input impedance (R_if = R_i / D).

cards.front: What does 'Shunt' at the output mean?
cards.back: Shunt sampling: feedback element connects directly to the output voltage node. Senses output voltage (V). Decreases output impedance (R_of = R_o / D).

cards.front: What does 'Series' at the output mean?
cards.back: Series sampling: feedback element is in series with the output current path. Senses output current (I). Increases output impedance (R_of = R_o × D).

cards.front: Impedance rules for all four topologies?
cards.back: Series input → R_i × D. Shunt input → R_i / D. Shunt output → R_o / D. Series output → R_o × D. Tip: 'Series' always multiplies, 'Shunt' always divides.

cards.front: How to identify topology from a circuit: 3-step method
cards.back: 1) Find the feedback element connecting output to input. 2) Check output connection: to output node (voltage/shunt) or in series with current (series)? 3) Check input connection: in parallel at base (shunt) or in series at emitter (series)?

cards.front: Why is the emitter follower classified as Series-Shunt?
cards.back: V_E appears in series with V_BE (series mixing at input). V_E = full output voltage fed back (shunt/voltage sampling at output). β = V_E/V_out = 1.

cards.front: Why does R_E without bypass capacitor in a CE stage create Series-Series feedback?
cards.back: R_E is in series with the emitter current (series output sampling of I_E ≈ I_C). V_E = I_E × R_E appears in series with V_BE (series input mixing). β = V_E/I_out.

cards.front: For Shunt-Shunt (transresistance), what is the unit and formula for β?
cards.back: β = I_f / V_o, units of 1/Ω (Siemens). For R_f from output to input: β = -1/R_f. Closed-loop gain R_Mf = R_M/(1+R_M·β) has units of Ohms.

cards.front: For Series-Series (transconductance), what is the unit and formula for β?
cards.back: β = V_f / I_o, units of Ohms. For emitter resistor R_E: β = R_E. Closed-loop gain G_Mf = G_M/(1+G_M·R_E), where G_M = h_fe/h_ie.

cards.front: What does 'loading effect' mean when breaking the feedback loop?
cards.back: The feedback network adds impedance loads to both the input and output of the amplifier, even when feedback is 'broken'. These loading effects must be included when calculating the open-loop gain A.
